title: antistrophe oxyantha
intro: Antistrophe oxyantha, also known as the forked fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a woody climber found in tropical Africa and Asia, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Antistrophe oxyantha has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
